entry.title: LGM Knowledge Snippet: Processing Variant Maintenance
entry.content:
In my previous post about Processing Variants, I explained the concept and how they control shipping process behavior in SAP Logistics Management. Now let's dive into the practical side: how do you actually configure and maintain Processing Variants using the Configure Shipping Processes app?

The Configure Shipping Processes App

The Configure Shipping Processes app (find the complete documentation here) is your central tool for managing Processing Variants. This Fiori application provides a clear interface for viewing SAP-delivered variants and creating custom ones tailored to your business requirements.

Viewing Existing Processing Variants

When you open the Configure Shipping Processes app, you'll see a list of all available Processing Variants-both the standard ones delivered by SAP and any custom variants your organization has created.

The list view provides several useful capabilities:

  • Filter by Shipping Process - Quickly narrow down to Parcel, LTL, Direct LTL, FTL, or No Shipping variants
  • Search by Display ID or Description - Find specific variants quickly in large catalogs
  • See SAP-Managed indicator - Immediately identify which variants are delivered by SAP vs. customer-created
  • Check Default status - See which variant is marked as the default fallback
  • View creation and modification details - Showing when variants were created and last changed

SAP delivers five standard Processing Variants out of the box:

  • /SAP/Parcel - Parcel shipments with carrier integrations
  • /SAP/LTL - Consolidated Less-Than-Truckload shipments
  • /SAP/Direct_LTL - Direct LTL with individual carrier confirmation
  • /SAP/No_Shipping - Warehouse-only scenarios

These delivered variants are read-only and serve as tested, stable baselines. You cannot modify them, but you can create your own custom variants based on the same Shipping Processes.

Creating a Custom Processing Variant

When your business has specific requirements that the standard variants don't address, you can create custom Processing Variants. Here's the step-by-step workflow:

Step 1: Start Creation

Click the "Create" button in the Configure Shipping Processes app. The system opens a new draft variant in edit mode.

Step 2: Select the Base Shipping Process

Choose which fundamental Shipping Process your variant will be based on:

  • Parcel - If you need special parcel carrier handling
  • LTL - For consolidated shipments with regular carrier schedules
  • Direct LTL - For shipments requiring explicit carrier confirmation
  • No Shipping - For warehouse-internal processes

This choice is critical because it determines which configuration options will be available in the next steps. The Shipping Process defines the foundational behavior and which attributes you can customize.

Step 3: Define the Display ID

Create a unique Display ID for your variant. Use a clear naming convention that indicates the purpose, for example:

  • "CUSTOM_LTL_EXPRESS" - For expedited LTL shipments
  • "CUSTOM_LTL_CONSOLIDATION" - For LTL with consolidation cutoff enabled
  • "CUSTOM_DIRECTLTL_MANUAL_CONFIRM" - For Direct LTL requiring manual carrier approval

Avoid generic names like "VARIANT_001" or "CUSTOM_1"-your operations team (and maybe also the SAP development team ;-)) will thank you months later when reviewing configurations or troubleshooting.

Step 4: Add a Description

Provide a clear, detailed description explaining:

  • What business scenario this variant supports
  • How it differs from standard SAP variants
  • Any special handling or prerequisites
  • Which organizational units or locations should use it

Note: So far, the delivered processing variants come with translated descriptions, this is not yet available for customer defined processing variants. 

Step 5: Configure Available Options

The app displays configuration options based on the Shipping Process you selected. Not all options are available for all processes-the system only shows settings that are meaningful for your chosen process type.

Currently there is only one option you can maintain (the idea here is of course, to add more step by step):

Consolidation Cutoff (available for LTL and Direct LTL)

Enable this when you want to consolidate multiple Transportation Requests into fewer Consignments during a defined time window. As I explained in the previous Processing Variants post, this dramatically changes process behavior:

  • The system searches for existing open Consignments when creating new Transportation Requests
  • Multiple requests get grouped into the same Consignment
  • Carrier determination happens after the consolidation window closes
  • A Consignment without a carrier is acceptable during consolidation but requires attention afterward

More details about consolidation cutoff can be found in the SAP Help Portal documentation.

The following picture shows an example oif a manually created Processing Variant:

 

 Step 6: Save as Draft

While working on your variant, you can save it as a draft. Draft variants are fully editable-you can change any configuration option, the Display ID, descriptions, or even the base Shipping Process.

Draft variants are not yet available for use in Transportation Requests or Consignments. Use the draft phase to validate your configuration before activation.

Step 7: Activate the Variant

Once you're satisfied with the configuration, activate the variant. This makes it available for:

  • Business rules that determine Processing Variants automatically
  • Manual selection when creating Transportation Requests
  • Assignment to Transportation Requests through APIs

Critical: After activation, the configuration options become immutable. You can still update descriptions and translations, but you cannot change which features are enabled or the base Shipping Process. This immutability ensures that active Transportation Requests and Consignments cannot be affected by configuration changes mid-process.

The Golden Rules of Processing Variant Maintenance

To ensure system stability and maintain trust in your logistics processes, Processing Variants follow strict governance rules:

Rule 1: Configuration Immutability After Activation

Once a Processing Variant is activated and referenced by any Transportation Request or Consignment, its configuration attributes are permanently locked. You cannot:

  • Change which Shipping Process it's based on
  • Enable or disable consolidation cutoff
  • Modify handling unit requirements
  • Alter any other process-controlling attributes

Why this restriction? Imagine you have 50 active Consignments using a variant configured for consolidation. If you could suddenly disable consolidation, those Consignments would be in an undefined state—the system wouldn't know whether to continue consolidating or stop. The immutability rule prevents such scenarios.

If you need to change a variant's configuration, create a new variant with your desired settings. Update your business rules to use the new variant for future Transportation Requests.

What You CAN Change After Activation:

  • Descriptions - Improve wording, add clarifications, fix typos
  • Default status - Mark a different variant as default

These changes don't affect process behavior for existing documents, so they're safe to modify at any time.

We already have ideas for a deprecation process for processing variants, but this will w´be introduced in a later step.

Rule 2: SAP-Managed Variants Are Completely Read-Only

The Processing Variants delivered by SAP (identifiable by Display IDs starting with "/SAP/") are completely read-only. You cannot modify:

  • Configuration options
  • Descriptions (including translations)
  • Display IDs
  • Any other attributes

SAP maintains these variants as tested, stable baselines. When SAP needs to update descriptions or add new translations, these changes come through system updates and migrations.

If an SAP-delivered variant almost meets your needs but requires one change, create a custom variant instead. Copy the concept, adjust the configuration, and give it your own Display ID.

Rule 3: Only One Default Variant

You can designate exactly one Processing Variant as the default. This variant serves as a fallback when:

  • Business rules cannot determine a specific variant
  • APIs create Transportation Requests without specifying a variant
  • Manual processes need a "safe" default choice

When you mark a variant as default using the "Set Default Processing Variant" action, the system automatically removes the default flag from whichever variant previously held it. There's no risk of accidentally having multiple defaults.

Choosing the Right Default:

Select a default variant that represents your most common or most flexible scenario. Typically, this means:

  • A variant without highly restrictive settings
  • One that applies to the broadest set of shipments
  • Your "standard" process that covers 70-80% of cases

The /SAP/No_Shipping variant is delivered as the default, but you'll likely want to change this to your primary shipping process variant once you've configured your business rules.

Setting the Default Processing Variant

To set a different variant as the default:

  1. Navigate to the Configure Shipping Processes app
  2. Select the variant you want to make default
  3. Click the "Set Default Processing Variant" action in the action toolbar
  4. The system updates the selected variant's default status and clears it from the previous default

The change takes effect immediately and applies to all future Transportation Requests where no specific variant is determined.

Troubleshooting Common Maintenance Scenarios

Scenario 1: "I need to change a configuration option on an active variant"

Solution: You cannot modify active variant configurations. Instead:

  1. Create a new custom variant with the desired configuration
  2. Give it a new Display ID (e.g., add "_V2" to indicate version 2)
  3. Update your business rules to use the new variant for future Transportation Requests
  4. Existing Transportation Requests continue using the old variant until completion
  5. Once all Transportation Requests using the old variant are complete, you can consider deprecating it

Scenario 2: "Which variant should I choose as the default?"

Solution: Analyze your Transportation Request creation patterns:

  • Which Shipping Process do you use most frequently?
  • What percentage of Transportation Requests can't be determined by business rules?
  • Which variant represents your "safest" fallback process?

For most customers, the default should be either /SAP/LTL or /SAP/Direct_LTL, depending on whether you typically consolidate shipments or handle them individually.

Scenario 3: "How do I know which variant a Transportation Request is using?"

Solution: The Processing Variant is displayed in the Transportation Request details. You can also filter Transportation Requests by Processing Variant in the Manage Transportation Requests app.

If you need to analyze variant usage at scale, the Processing Variant field is available in reporting and analytics.

Scenario 4: "Can I delete a Processing Variant that's no longer needed?"

Solution: Deletion of Processing Variants is not currently supported through the UI. Here's why:

  • Historical Transportation Requests reference the variant
  • Reporting and analytics need the variant definition
  • Audit trails require variant information

Instead of deletion, consider creating clear naming conventions that indicate deprecated variants (e.g., prefixing with "DEPRECATED_") and updating descriptions to indicate they should no longer be used.

Integration with Business Rules

While the Configure Shipping Processes app lets you create and maintain Processing Variants, the real power comes from integrating them with business rules for automatic determination.

In the Business Rules configuration, you can define rules like:

  • "For Transportation Requests from Shipping Point 1000 with weight < 500kg → use CUSTOM_LTL_CONSOLIDATION"
  • "For express orders → use CUSTOM_LTL_EXPRESS"

The Processing Variants you create in the Configure Shipping Processes app become available as targets in these business rules. This separation of concerns is powerful:

  • Processing Variants define what each process variation does
  • Business Rules define when each variant should be used

You can change business rules (when to use a variant) without affecting variant configurations (what the variant does).

Wrapping Up

The Configure Shipping Processes app puts powerful configuration capabilities at your fingertips, letting you tailor SAP Logistics Management to your exact business requirements without coding. By understanding the maintenance workflows, governance rules, and best practices, you can confidently create and manage Processing Variants that support your logistics operations.

Key takeaways for maintaining Processing Variants:

  • Start with SAP-delivered variants and only create custom ones when truly needed
  • Use clear, descriptive Display IDs and comprehensive descriptions
  • Remember that configurations are immutable after activation—test thoroughly first
  • Integrate with business rules for automatic, intelligent variant determination
  • Maintain documentation and review periodically to prevent configuration drift

Have you created custom Processing Variants in your LGM implementation? What configuration options would you like to see added in future releases? Share your experiences and suggestions in the comments!
